description_long: This kiosk is a small, standalone masonry structure with weathered, aged walls and a partially ruined roof. The front features a pointed Gothic-style arched doorway, currently open and leading into a dark, empty interior. The exterior walls show significant wear, with patches of exposed brickwork and faded plaster, suggesting long-term exposure to the elements. There is a small, rectangular window on one side near the roofline, adding to the utilitarian yet historic appearance. The roof is mostly flat but damaged, with missing sections and visible debris. The structure sits on a slightly raised, grassy base, blending into an urban or park environment.
description: A small, weathered masonry kiosk with a Gothic arched doorway, exposed brickwork, and a partially ruined flat roof. Features a single side window and sits on a grassy base.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the Gothic arched doorway, weathered blue-gray wall, and partial roof damage.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the plain, faded wall, exposed brick, and a small rectangular window near the roof.
description_view_2: Interior-facing view with a clear look at the arched doorway and the worn, pale wall surfaces.
description_view_3: Side view emphasizing the depth of the structure, the arched entrance, and the extent of roof damage.
attribute_window_presence: one small rectangular window on the left side near the roof
attribute_door_configuration: single open arched doorway at the front
attribute_roof_design: mostly flat roof with significant damage and missing sections, constructed from masonry materials
